meta.name: Broccoli Cheddar Soup
meta.subtitle: Creamy broccoli + sharp cheddar • cafe-style vibe (Panera-inspired feel, not a copy)

source.notes: Original formulation designed for a Panera-style texture and flavor: creamy base, broccoli-forward, carrot/onion notes, sharp cheddar finish. Not a proprietary copy.

yield.notes: Makes ~14–16 cups depending on broccoli size and final thickness.

recommended: box grater (if shredding your own cheese)

tasteProfile.notes: Creamy, cheddar-forward soup with broccoli bite and subtle sweetness from carrot/onion.

ingredients.label: Carrots, diced small
ingredients.notes: About 2 medium. Small dice helps 'cafe-style' texture.

ingredients.label: Celery, diced small (optional)
ingredients.notes: About 2 stalks. Adds classic soup-base aroma.

ingredients.label: Chicken broth or vegetable broth (low-sodium preferred)
Heavy cream (optional, extra rich)
ingredients.notes: About 1/2 cup. If using, reduce milk slightly or keep total liquid the same.

ingredients.label: Dry mustard powder (optional, boosts cheddar flavor)
Ground nutmeg (optional)
ingredients.notes: A tiny pinch adds 'cafe' cream-soup depth.

ingredients.label: Broccoli florets (chopped bite-size) + peeled stems (optional, diced)
ingredients.notes: About 1.25–1.5 lb. Include diced stems for less waste.

ingredients.label: Sharp cheddar cheese, freshly shredded
ingredients.notes: About 12 oz. Freshly shredded melts smoother than pre-shredded.

ingredients.label: Salt
ingredients.notes: Start with ~1 1/4 teaspoons; adjust after cheese melts.

ingredients.label: Hot sauce (optional, for balance)
ingredients.notes: A few dashes. Helps cut richness without making it spicy.

steps.title: Sweat vegetables
instructions: Melt butter in a large pot over medium heat.
Add onion, carrots, and celery (if using). Cook 6–8 minutes until softened.
Add garlic and cook 30 seconds until fragrant.

steps.title: Make the roux (thickener)
instructions: Sprinkle flour over the vegetables and stir continuously for 1–2 minutes to cook out raw flour taste.
The mixture should look thick and slightly pasty (roux coating the vegetables).

steps.title: Add liquids and seasonings; simmer
instructions: Slowly whisk in broth to avoid lumps, then whisk in milk (and cream if using).
Add mustard powder/paprika/nutmeg (optional), salt, and pepper.
Bring to a gentle simmer (do not boil hard). Simmer 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until slightly thickened.

steps.title: Cook broccoli until tender
instructions: Add chopped broccoli (and diced stems if using).
Simmer 10–12 minutes until broccoli is tender but not mushy.

steps.title: Optional: blend a portion for cafe-style texture
instructions: For a smoother cafe-style soup, blend briefly with an immersion blender (5–10 seconds bursts) to puree part of the broccoli and thicken the soup.
Alternatively, ladle 2 cups into a blender, puree, then return to pot. (Vent blender carefully.)

steps.title: Melt in cheddar off heat
instructions: Remove pot from heat (important to prevent grainy cheese).
Add cheddar in small handfuls, stirring until fully melted before adding more.
Return to very low heat only if needed to keep warm—avoid boiling.

steps.title: Finish and serve
instructions: Add optional hot sauce and/or lemon juice to brighten and balance richness.
Serve hot, especially good with toasted bread or in a bread bowl.

broccoli: Frozen broccoli works: add straight from frozen; simmer a few minutes longer.
If broccoli is very coarse, chop smaller for a more 'cafe-style' bite.
cheese: Use sharp cheddar for best flavor. Avoid pre-shredded if possible (anti-caking agents can make it grainy).
If substituting: use a mix of sharp cheddar + mild cheddar for a less sharp finish.
thickness: If too thick: add a splash of milk or broth.
If too thin: simmer a few extra minutes before adding cheese, or blend more broccoli.
grainyPrevention: Keep heat gentle; never boil after adding cheese.
Add cheese off heat and in handfuls.

bestPractices: Best fresh. If freezing, expect some texture change due to dairy—stir well when reheating.
For best freezer results: freeze the base before adding cheese, then add cheese after thawing/reheating.

storage.fridge: Refrigerate up to 4 days. Reheat gently (low heat) to avoid separating.
storage.freezer: Freeze up to 2 months. Dairy soups may separate; reheat slowly and whisk to re-emulsify. Best practice: freeze base without cheese and add cheese after thawing.
storage.reheat: Warm on low, stirring frequently. Do not boil. Add a splash of milk/broth if needed.

audit.testNotes: Schema-compliant: schemaVersion+meta present; all ingredients include id+label+amount; all steps include id+title+instructions; no disallowed top-level properties per current recipe.schema.json.
